title: Multi-Scale Spatio-Temporal Event Description Schema (metropolis-v3.0)
description: Schema for msted_*.json - Multi-Scale Spatio-Temporal Event Description (TAO DAFT metropolis-v3.0). MSTED is an auto-generated annotation that captures scene context, temporal/spatial localization, and structured event characterization at multiple scales.

version.description: Schema version - must be '3.0' for this schema

metadata.description: File metadata for identification and cataloging

description.description: Human-readable description of this annotation file

license.description: License under which this annotation is distributed

tags.description: Dataset-level tags for categorization and search

video_id.description: Reference to video file (matches video_id in video_*.json)

sources.description: List of contextual files used to generate this MSTED (e.g. video_human.json, events_human.json, chunks_auto.json)

scene_description.description: Holistic Scene Description: environment, weather, time of day, scene layout, and pre-event baseline behavior — establishing the normal operational context so that the event of focus can be accurately characterized relative to that baseline.

temporal_spatial_localization.description: Temporal and Spatial Localization: a chronological narrative of the event's progression with precise start/end times and spatial region, anchoring all downstream annotations to verifiable evidence in the video.

start.description: Segment start time in flexible timecode: MM:SS or HH:MM:SS with optional fractional seconds (e.g. '00:02', '00:02.70', '00:01:30')

end.description: Segment end time in flexible timecode: MM:SS or HH:MM:SS with optional fractional seconds (e.g. '00:04', '00:04.50', '00:01:35')

description.description: Chronological narrative of what occurs in this temporal segment

spatial_region.description: Spatial location within the frame or scene where this segment occurs

event_description.description: Event of Focus Description: a structured characterization of the salient event identified in the video. Key names are flexible — use whatever fields best describe the event. All values must be strings. Suggested fields: category, description, temporal_description, spatial_location, cause, consequence.

examples.scene_description: The video captures a high-angle view of a busy urban arterial road on a sunny day. The scene features a five-lane, two-way thoroughfare with vehicles flowing in both directions.

temporal_spatial_localization.description: Traffic flows normally. A white sedan travels in lane 5, a gray sedan in lane 3.
The gray sedan initiates a left turn from lane 3, colliding with the white sedan in lane 5.

event_description.description: A gray sedan attempted a left turn from a middle lane, failing to yield to a white sedan traveling straight in the outer lane.
event_description.temporal_description: The primary collision occurs between frames 81 and 135, with secondary impact immediately after.
event_description.spatial_location: Travel lanes of Rainier Ave S near the intersection.
event_description.cause: Improper left turn by the gray sedan, which failed to ensure the lane was clear.
event_description.consequence: Property damage to both vehicles and a roadside tree. The white sedan was disabled on the sidewalk.
